title: nvm_list infinite-loop guard comment obscures server contract
description: The comment on the out_id == 0 guard in nvm_list reads: 'Safety guard against an infinite loop: if the server returns id 0 after a success response, start_id would not advance and the next iteration would send the same query forever.' This frames it as a Rust-side defensive measure, but out_id == 0 means the server returned WH_NVM_ID_INVALID as the next object ID — that is a server contract violation, not a normal condition we are gracefully handling. A reader who does not know the wolfHSM API will not understand why id 0 is special or how serious this is. What would make it clear: rewrite as 'out_id == 0 is WH_NVM_ID_INVALID — the server should never return this as a valid object ID. If it does, the list is corrupt; break rather than looping forever on the same query.' Optionally return an error instead of silently truncating the list.
